en_title: Paramount and Warner Bros. Merger Paused for at Least 2 Weeks by Judge
cn_title: 法官下令派拉蒙与华纳兄弟合并暂停两周
subtitle: 反垄断争议

paragraphs.en: A judge has ordered a temporary pause to the controversial Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.
paragraphs.cn: 一名法官下令暂时叫停备受争议的派拉蒙与华纳兄弟合并。

paragraphs.en: As reported by Variety , Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin called for the merger to come to a stop after a state coalition argued the move would violate federal antitrust law. The fear is that it could lead to higher prices and fewer movies and TV shows.
paragraphs.cn: 据《综艺》报道，在一个州联盟主张交易违反联邦反垄断法后，法官Araceli Martinez-Olguin要求暂停合并。各方担忧交易可能导致价格上涨、电影和电视剧数量减少。

paragraphs.en: Paramount had reportedly expected to close the deal by Wednesday, July 22. The temporary restraining order will keep the deal from being finalized for another 14 days, but could be extended for as much as 28 days.
paragraphs.cn: 据报派拉蒙原计划在7月22日星期三完成交易。临时限制令将阻止交易在未来14天内完成，并可能延长至最多28天。

paragraphs.en: A total of 12 states seek to block the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger. Paramount attorney Jeffrey Kessler has argued the states have failed to provide enough evidence to suggest the merger is anticompetitive.
paragraphs.cn: 共有12个州寻求阻止派拉蒙与华纳兄弟合并。派拉蒙律师Jeffrey Kessler称，各州未能提供足够证据证明交易具有反竞争性。

paragraphs.en: "Plaintiff States' showing at least demonstrates that serious questions going to the merits remain, weighing in favor of preliminary injunctive relief," Martinez-Olguin said. "Paramount and Warner Bros. will continue to operate as separate, viable companies competing in the marketplace while they wait for the Court to adjudicate this case. The balance of equities, combined with the public’s vital interest in antitrust enforcement, therefore tips sharply in favor of the requested injunctive relief."
paragraphs.cn: Martinez-Olguin表示：「原告各州的陈述至少表明，案件实质仍存在重大问题，因此有利于给予初步禁令救济。法院审理本案期间，派拉蒙和华纳兄弟将继续作为独立且有生存能力的公司在市场竞争。衡平因素与公众对反垄断执法的重要利益相结合，因此明显支持所请求的禁令救济。」

paragraphs.en: A Paramount takeover of Warner Bros. was almost certainly always going to have its critics, but the lead-up to today's events has been especially controversial. Movie fans will remember when Netflix announced an $82.7 billion deal to acquire the company back in December 2025. The months ahead saw that slowly fall apart after Paramount revealed it had launched its own hostile bid , which it called a "superior all-cash offer" at the time.
paragraphs.cn: 派拉蒙收购华纳兄弟几乎注定会招致批评，但发展到今天的过程尤其充满争议。影迷应该还记得，Netflix曾在2025年12月宣布以82.7 billion，即827亿美元(约合人民币5607亿元)收购华纳兄弟。此后数月，随着派拉蒙宣布发起敌意竞购，并称其为「更优越的全现金报价」，Netflix的交易逐渐瓦解。

paragraphs.en: In a February 2026 plot twist, it was reported that Warner Bros. was interested in reopening negotiations with Paramount despite agreeing to sell to Netflix. Then, later that same month, Netflix walked away from the deal , all but securing Paramount as the victor with its $110 billion offer. Actually finalizing the deal, however, is a completely different story.
paragraphs.cn: 2026年2月，剧情出现转折：尽管华纳兄弟已同意出售给Netflix，据报它仍有意重启与派拉蒙的谈判。同月晚些时候，Netflix退出交易，派拉蒙凭借110 billion，即1100亿美元(约合人民币7458亿元)的报价几乎锁定胜局。然而，真正完成交易又是另一回事。

paragraphs.en: As noted by NBC News , the suit to block the merger was launched July 13, with the court hearing arguments from both the 12-state coalition and Paramount Friday, July 17. California Attorney General Rob Bonta called the judge's ruling a "critical first win in our case to ensure this megamerger never sees the light of day."
paragraphs.cn: 据NBC新闻报道，阻止合并的诉讼于7月13日发起，法院在7月17日星期五听取了12州联盟与派拉蒙双方的陈述。加州总检察长Rob Bonta称，法官的裁决是「本案至关重要的首场胜利，确保这场超级合并永远无法见天日」。

paragraphs.en: "History tells the tale of what happens when a few people have great power over markets that are central to Americans' lives: fewer opportunities for more people, worse products and services for all people," Bonta said (via Variety). "With our lawsuit, we’re fighting for a free and fair market and a thriving film and television industry that serves creatives and audiences alike. We have a full tank of gas, the law on our side, and look forward to continuing to make our case."
paragraphs.cn: Bonta通过《综艺》表示：「历史告诉我们，当少数人对美国人生活中至关重要的市场掌握巨大权力时，会发生什么：更多人获得的机会更少，所有人得到的产品和服务更差。通过这场诉讼，我们正在为自由公平的市场、以及同时服务创作者与观众的繁荣影视行业而战。我们动力充足，法律也站在我们这边，期待继续陈述我们的理由。」

paragraphs.en: He added (via NBC): "The unlawful merger of these two entertainment behemoths would lead to higher prices, lower quality, and less content for film and television, harming movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and ultimately, audiences on every sofa and movie theater seat in the U.S."
paragraphs.cn: 他又通过NBC表示：「这两家娱乐巨头的非法合并将导致价格上涨、质量下降、影视内容减少，损害电影院、基础有线电视发行商，并最终伤害美国每张沙发和每个影院座位上的观众。」

paragraphs.en: Paramount, meanwhile, has fought back against the claims. In a statement, it called the restraining order "one of the weakest merger challenges in modern antitrust history."
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，派拉蒙对这些指控展开反击。该公司在声明中称，这项限制令是「现代反垄断史上最站不住脚的合并挑战之一」。

paragraphs.en: "We will vigorously defend the transaction and demonstrate that this challenge is inconsistent with sound competition policy and the competitive realities of the media marketplace," Paramount added.
paragraphs.cn: 派拉蒙补充道：「我们将积极捍卫这项交易，并证明这项挑战既不符合合理的竞争政策，也不符合媒体市场的竞争现实。」

opus_summary: 美国法官应12州请求，暂时叫停派拉蒙与华纳兄弟合并。争议交易经历Netflix退出、派拉蒙1100亿美元(约合人民币7458亿元)竞购；各州警告合并将推高价格、降低质量，派拉蒙则称诉讼缺乏依据。
